heading: Creation of remedy
label: 2201 Creation of remedy
notes: Amendment of SectionFor termination of amendment by , see Effective and Termination Dates of 1988 Amendment note below.section 501(c) of Pub. L. 100–449

1948ActBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 400 (, as added , ; , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 274dJune 14, 1934, ch. 51248 Stat. 955Aug. 30, 1935, ch. 829, § 40549 Stat. 1027This section is based on the first paragraph of , U.S.C., 1940 ed. Other provisions of such section are incorporated in .section 400 of title 28section 2202 of this titleWhile this section does not exclude declaratory judgments with respect to State taxes, such suits will not ordinarily be entertained in the courts of the United States where State law makes provision for payment under protest and recovery back or otherwise affords adequate remedy in the State courts. See , La. 1943, 63 S.Ct. 1070, 319 U.S. 293, 87 L.Ed. 1407. See also , Conn. 1944, 65 S.Ct. 152, 323 U.S. 101, 89 L.Ed. 101. See also forbidding district courts to restrain enforcements of State taxes where State courts afford plain, speedy, and efficient remedy.Great Lakes Dredge & Dock Co. v. HuffmanSpector Motor Service v. McLaughlinsection 1341 of this titleChanges were made in phraseology.
1949ActSection corrects a typographical error in , U.S.C.section 2201 of title 28

children.label: a
children.text: In a case of actual controversy within its jurisdiction, except with respect to Federal taxes other than actions brought under section 7428 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, a proceeding under section 505 or 1146 of title 11, or in any civil action involving an antidumping or countervailing duty proceeding regarding a class or kind of merchandise of a free trade area country (as defined in section 516A(f)(9) of the Tariff Act of 1930), as determined by the administering authority, any court of the United States, upon the filing of an appropriate pleading, may declare the rights and other legal relations of any interested party seeking such declaration, whether or not further relief is or could be sought. Any such declaration shall have the force and effect of a final judgment or decree and shall be reviewable as such.

children.label: b
children.text: For limitations on actions brought with respect to drug patents see section 505 or 512 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, or section 351 of the Public Health Service Act.
